rationale: Qalb is an Arabic Lisp-like programming language by Ramsey Nasser (2012). Arabic and Urdu share the Perso-Arabic script and many loanwords. While different language families (Semitic vs. Indo-Aryan), shared script exposes the model to programming concepts in a script Urdu speakers read. Used as native-PL component of Condition 3-ur.
quality_filter: all files syntactically valid (official examples from source repo or verified wiki samples); no duplicates

files.description: Recursive Fibonacci — defines and calls fibonacci(10)
Conway's Game of Life — full implementation with Gosper glider gun seed (41×19 grid)
REPL startup script — prints banner, defines help/examples navigation in Arabic
Conditional example — if/greater-than/say (embedded snippet from index.html)
Truth machine — prints input, loops if input is 1
Collatz sequence — prints steps of Collatz conjecture for n=25

corpus_notes: This is the complete publicly available corpus of Qalb source code as of 2026-04-03.
The nasser/--- repo is primarily a JavaScript interpreter; .qlb example files are sparse.
Rosetta Code lists Qalb but returns 403 for automated access; no additional tasks retrieved.
SourceHut (git.sr.ht/~nasser/) returns 403 for unauthenticated access; may contain additional files.
GitHub code search for .qlb requires auth; manual search found no additional community repos with .qlb files.
7 files is at the low end of 'possibly dozens' but represents the complete verified public corpus.
For Condition 3-ur blend, Qalb will be combined with Legesher-transpiled Urdu Python and community data.
